generated_comment:
A: Arre bhai, aadmi se galti ho jaati hai, par jo aurat jaan ke married aadmi ke saath chipakti hai na, wahi asli ghar todne wali hoti hai.
B: Haan, par uski bhi responsibility hai.
A: Responsibility toh hai, par aisi auratein hi phasati hain, bilkul serial wali baat hai. Bechara ghar barbaad ho jaata hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames the woman as the primary cause of an affair while minimizing the man's agency as a forgivable mistake. It encodes blame asymmetry, 'homewrecker' framing, and seduction/victimhood language like 'phasati hain' and 'bechara ghar barbaad ho jaata hai'.
original_frame: Misogyny Jokes: Period, Hormones and Mood.
persona_text: You are Sanjay. Age 37. A man in a middle class colony in Delhi. You are sitting with four male neighbours on plastic chairs outside after dinner. One neighbour is complaining that his wife has been in a terrible mood all week. You are responding with your own theory about why women get this way and what the men in the group should do about it.
